case_name: Choice Clinical Lab, LLC D/B/A Greenleaves Diagnostics Laboratories, LLC v. Olga Roman Roberts, Individually and as Independent of the Estate of Aubrey Roberts, and Grace Robinson, Abigail Roberts and Aubrey Roberts, III Individually

summary.brief: Choice Clinical Lab, LLC D/B/A Greenleaves Diagnostics Laboratories, LLC v. Olga Roman Roberts, Individually and as Independent of the Estate of Aubrey Roberts, and Grace Robinson, Abigail Roberts and Aubrey Roberts, III Individually. Medical malpractice case involving oncology.

evidence_index.text: On March 8, 2022, Aubrey Roberts was admitted to Methodist Stone Oak Hospital to treat injuries after a fall. He underwent surgery to repair an 'acute right femoral neck fracture.'
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: At the time of his admission to Coronado, he had been diagnosed with unspecified dementia, allergic rhinitis, vitamin deficiency, history of falls, insomnia, shortness of breath, flaccid neuropathic bladder, mild protein-calorie malnutrition, Alzheimer’s disease, retention of urine, dysphagia, COPD, constipation, and recent fracture of femur.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The staff at Coronado took urine and blood samples from Aubrey Roberts and sent them to Choice Clinical for testing.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr. Saba’s expert report further states how the breaches of care by Coronado and Choice Clinical caused Aubrey Roberts’s death: As a result of the breaches of the standard of care as described above[,] Mr. Roberts suffered from choking, over medication, and UTI, which was allowed to fester without sufficient care and treatment until it resulted in septic shock and his subsequent cardiac arrest, hypotensive state, and death.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Saba

evidence_index.text: We therefore conclude the trial court did not abuse its discretion in determining that Dr. Saba was qualified to give his expert opinion regarding the standard of care applicable to Choice Clinical.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: Because the expert report sufficiently showed Dr. Saba was qualified to give his expert opinion regarding the standard of care applicable to Choice Clinical, we hold the trial court did not abuse its discretion in overruling Choice Clinical’s objections to Dr. Saba’s expert report.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Aubrey Roberts was admitted to Methodist Stone Oak Hospital on March 8, 2022, to treat injuries after a fall, including an acute right femoral neck fracture. He underwent surgery and was discharged to Coronado for rehabilitation and therapy on March 11, 2022.

focused_exam.system: Examination mentioned but findings not specified

initial_state.initial_working_problem: Rehabilitation post-surgery for femoral neck fracture

reveal.clinical_significance: Indicates bacterial infection requiring specific antibiotics
reveal.decision_impact: Should prompt immediate communication to healthcare provider

timeline_phases.name: Hospital Admission
timeline_phases.description: Patient admitted to hospital for fall-related injuries

timeline_phases.name: Rehabilitation at Coronado
timeline_phases.description: Patient transferred to Coronado for rehabilitation

key_events.event: Lab samples sent to Choice Clinical

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient at Coronado with suspected UTI
decision_points.prompt: When should lab results be communicated to the healthcare provider?

options.description: Communicate lab results immediately upon receipt
options.clinical_reasoning: Timely communication is critical for appropriate treatment
Delay can lead to inappropriate treatment and worsening of condition

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant delayed communication of lab results until April 13, 2022

expected_action_court.description: Lab results should have been communicated immediately upon receipt on April 8, 2022
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert evidence established immediate communication is standard

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Delay in communication led to a significant delay in appropriate treatment
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Lab results should have been communicated immediately to allow for correct treatment
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Standard of care as established by expert testimony

patient_outcome.description: Patient developed septic shock and died

key_findings: Breach of duty in failing to timely communicate lab results
Causation established - delay in communication led to inappropriate treatment and death

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Failure to timely communicate lab results
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Immediate communication would have allowed for correct treatment

factual_timeline.event: Aubrey Roberts admitted to Methodist Stone Oak Hospital

allegations.allegation: Failure to timely communicate lab results

court_findings.finding: Choice Clinical breached duty of care
court_findings.legal_significance: Breach led to delay in treatment and death
